world.title: 'Blew a hole in engine room': How US forces seized Iranian ship 'Touska' after six
world.summary: US forces seized the Iranian-flagged cargo vessel Touska in the Arabian Sea during a high-risk operation on Monday. Visuals from US Central Command showed Marines rappelling onto the ship after it was disabled by naval fire. The operation highlights ongoing tensions in the region, as US forces continue to monitor and respond to maritime threats.

world.title: Trump kept out of war room, 'screamed at aides': What happened after US jet was shot down in Iran
world.summary: A US jet was shot down over Iran, causing turmoil in the White House. President Donald Trump reportedly screamed at aides and was excluded from the war room where officials discussed a high-risk rescue mission. The incident highlighted the intense pressure and chaos surrounding the situation, as reported by The Wall Street Journal.

world.title: US admiral explains Trump’s ‘brilliant’ plan that will force Iran to concede defeat
world.summary: The United States is implementing a strategy aimed at compelling Iran to abandon its nuclear ambitions and restore free navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Former national security official Robert Harward described this approach as a coordinated military and economic campaign that is already placing significant pressure on Tehran, potentially leading to Iran's capitulation in the ongoing conflict.

business.title: 2026 Kia Syros Launched - Prices Slashed, 3 New Trims, Revised Styling
business.summary: Kia India launched the 2026 Syros, a sub 4m SUV featuring updated exterior styling and a revised variant lineup. The new model offers three trims and a lower base price, aiming to enhance sales in the competitive segment. It is equipped exclusively with Turbo Petrol engines, positioning it as a premium option in the Indian SUV market.

business.title: Natural Gas News: Weather-Driven Demand Supports Natural Gas Market Today
business.summary: Natural gas prices increased as hot weather from April 22–26 heightened cooling demand. Despite this rise, strong production and high inventory levels have limited significant gains, maintaining a balanced market outlook. May Nymex Natural Gas has edged higher for four consecutive sessions; however, this upward movement has not altered the ongoing three-month downtrend in prices.

business.title: Blackstone dialing Blackstone: AirTrunk eyes India entry with Lumina CloudInfra buyout
business.summary: AirTrunk, a hyperscale data centre platform backed by Blackstone, is in final negotiations to acquire Lumina CloudInfra, an India-focused developer of large-scale data centres. The acquisition aims to enhance AirTrunk's presence in India. Sources indicate that AirTrunk plans to invest up to $5 billion into Lumina’s portfolio over time, expanding its operations in the region.

technology.title: Samsung's image editing app gets a massive redesign, new features
technology.summary: Samsung has launched a significant update for its image editing app, Galaxy Enhance-X. The redesign introduces three distinct tabs, enhancing user navigation. Additionally, the update includes numerous new features aimed at improving the editing experience. This overhaul reflects Samsung's commitment to providing advanced tools for users, making image editing more efficient and user-friendly.

technology.title: Has Apple already begun marketing Siri's big overhaul? Check hidden AI clues in WWDC 2026 poster
technology.summary: Apple will unveil iOS 27 at WWDC 2026 in June. A report by Bloomberg's Mark Gurman indicates that the event's teaser poster contains hints about a significant overhaul of the Siri assistant. This suggests that Apple is preparing to enhance Siri's capabilities, with details expected to be revealed during the upcoming conference.

technology.title: Motorola Edge 70 Pro: Check India launch date, expected price, full specs, and 50MP camera with Super AMOLED display
technology.summary: Motorola is set to launch the Edge 70 Pro in India, featuring a 50MP camera, a curved Super AMOLED display, and a large battery. The device aims to compete in the mid-premium smartphone market with a flagship-grade chipset. While official details remain unconfirmed, early leaks suggest a strong emphasis on camera performance and display quality.

sports.title: Lalit Modi Makes Shock 'Black Magic In IPL' Claim, Says A Team Owner Did So In 2011
sports.summary: Lalit Modi, former IPL chairman, claimed that an IPL team owner engaged in "black magic" during the 2011 season. This statement emerged after a video of a Sunrisers Hyderabad fan performing a similar act went viral. The Sunrisers recently celebrated a victory over the Chennai Super Kings, shifting the focus from gameplay to the controversy surrounding Modi's allegations.

sports.title: The making of India’s 95th GM: Aronyak Ghosh, from chasing next tournament’s fee to parents’ relief
sports.summary: Aronyak Ghosh, now India's 95th Grandmaster, began his chess journey at age four when he found a box of chess pieces during a cleaning session. His passion for the game grew, leading him to compete in various tournaments. Ghosh's achievement brings relief to his parents, who supported his chess career, highlighting the dedication and effort required to reach this milestone.

sports.title: German model LizLaz breaks silence on Virat Kohli liking her picture: 'Feel sorry for him and...'
sports.summary: German model LizLaz addressed the controversy surrounding Indian cricketer Virat Kohli liking her picture on social media. The incident sparked significant online debate, reminiscent of a previous situation involving actress Avneet Kaur. LizLaz expressed sympathy for Kohli, suggesting that he might be facing undue scrutiny. The situation highlights the impact of social media interactions on public perception.

health.title: Why Losing Weight In 2026 Feels Harder Than Ever In India
health.summary: In 2026, weight loss in India is increasingly challenging due to various factors. Experts highlight the impact of biological predispositions, the prevalence of ultra-processed foods, sedentary lifestyles, and the effects of urban living. These elements contribute significantly to the country's escalating obesity crisis, making it harder for individuals to achieve and maintain weight loss goals.

health.title: WKH on alert after B’desh measles outbreak
health.summary: The West Khasi Hills district administration is on alert following measles outbreaks in Bangladesh. An order issued by the Deputy Commissioner on April 16 emphasizes the need for enhanced early detection and response mechanisms. It mandates immediate reporting of all fever with rash cases, suspected to be measles, across all age groups to mitigate potential risks in border areas.

health.title: Marengo Asia Hospitals inaugurates Center for Functional Neurosurgery on World Parkinson’s Day
health.summary: Marengo Asia Hospitals in Gurugram inaugurated its Center for Functional Neurosurgery on World Parkinson’s Day. The event included an awareness campaign focusing on early diagnosis, advanced treatments, and long-term care for Parkinson’s disease. The center aims to enhance treatment options for patients, emphasizing the importance of understanding the condition and improving quality of life through specialized care.

science.title: Shooting Stars responsible for Venusian haze
science.summary: New research indicates that the hazy lower atmosphere of Venus is primarily caused by cosmic dust, specifically fragments from shooting stars. Scientists from Tohoku University in Japan, in collaboration with the Royal Belgian Institute for Space Aeronomy, conducted the study, enhancing understanding of Venus's atmospheric composition. This finding may provide insights into the planet's environmental conditions and evolution.

science.title: Watch: Astronaut shares once-in-a-lifetime video of Earth setting behind the Moon
science.summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, commander of the Artemis 2 mission, shared a 53-second iPhone video on social media platform X, showcasing Earth setting behind the Moon. This rare footage provides a personal glimpse into space exploration, highlighting the awe-inspiring sights encountered during the mission. The video captures a unique moment in history, reflecting the advancements in space technology and exploration.

science.title: The Elements That Built Us: Rewriting How Dying Stars Seeded the Cosmos
science.summary: X-ray light from a cluster of over a thousand galaxies has prompted astrophysicists to revise key theories regarding element formation in the universe. Data from Japan's Hitomi telescope revealed discrepancies in standard models of what massive stars produce during their final moments. This finding challenges existing understanding and suggests a more complex process in the cosmos's elemental creation.

entertainment.title: 'Raaka' director Atlee and wife Priya welcome baby girl as Samantha Ruth Prabhu and Deepika Padukone shower love
entertainment.summary: Director Atlee and his wife Priya have welcomed their second child, a baby girl, born on April 20, 2026. The announcement was made on social media, where it quickly gained attention. Their son, Meer, is excited about his new sister. Atlee and Priya have been married since 2014 and are already parents to one child.

entertainment.title: When Abhishek Bachchan And Aishwarya Rai Fight, Who Says Sorry First? Her Answer
entertainment.summary: Aishwarya Rai and Abhishek Bachchan are celebrating their 19th wedding anniversary today, having married on April 20, 2007. They are parents to a daughter named Aaradhya Bachchan. In a previous appearance on The Kapil Sharma Show, Aishwarya discussed their relationship dynamics, including who typically apologizes first after disagreements, although specific details from that discussion were not included.

entertainment.title: Deepika Padukone films action-packed Raaka and King while expecting second baby, redefining motherhood
entertainment.summary: Deepika Padukone is currently filming action roles in "Raaka" and "King" while expecting her second child. Her work during pregnancy is prompting discussions about the intersection of motherhood and a demanding career in Indian cinema. Padukone's commitment to her projects challenges traditional notions of motherhood, showcasing that it is possible to balance professional and personal responsibilities.
